    *   *Drafting:*
        The Industrial Revolution fundamentally reshaped the landscape of Europe, marking a turning point in history. Economically, it signaled a transition from an agrarian, handicraft-based economy to one dominated by machine manufacturing and the factory system. This shift facilitated mass production, lowered the cost of goods, and spurred the growth of global trade and capitalism.

        Socially, the impact was equally profound. The period saw massive urbanization as rural populations migrated to cities in search of work, leading to the rapid growth of industrial centers. This demographic shift created a new social hierarchy: the rise of the industrial middle class (the bourgeoisie), who owned the means of production, and the emergence of a distinct urban working class (the proletariat). While it led to technological progress and a higher standard of living for some, it also brought harsh realities like poor living conditions, long working hours, and child labor, eventually sparking labor movements and the rise of socialism and communism.
